name: api-and-interface-design
description_raw: Guides stable API and interface design. Use when designing APIs, module boundaries, or any public interface. Use when creating REST or GraphQL endpoints, defining type contracts between modules, or establishing boundaries between frontend and backend.
description_normalized: Guides stable, hard-to-misuse API and interface design across REST, GraphQL, module boundaries, and component contracts.

frontmatter_yaml.name: api-and-interface-design
frontmatter_yaml.description: Guides stable API and interface design. Use when designing APIs, module boundaries, or any public interface. Use when creating REST or GraphQL endpoints, defining type contracts between modules, or establishing boundaries between frontend and backend.

name: browser-testing-with-devtools
description_raw: Tests in real browsers via Chrome DevTools MCP. Use when building or debugging anything that runs in a browser. Use when you need to inspect the DOM, capture console errors, analyze network requests, profile performance, or verify visual output with real runtime data. Requires the chrome-devtools MCP server to be configured.
description_normalized: Uses Chrome DevTools MCP to give agents live browser inspection — DOM, console, network, and performance — instead of static code analysis.

frontmatter_yaml.name: browser-testing-with-devtools
frontmatter_yaml.description: Tests in real browsers via Chrome DevTools MCP. Use when building or debugging anything that runs in a browser. Use when you need to inspect the DOM, capture console errors, analyze network requests, profile performance, or verify visual output with real runtime data. Requires the chrome-devtools MCP server to be configured.

name: ci-cd-and-automation
description_raw: Automates CI/CD pipeline setup. Use when setting up or modifying build and deployment pipelines. Use when you need to automate quality gates, configure test runners in CI, or establish deployment strategies.
description_normalized: Sets up and modifies CI/CD pipelines to automate quality gates, test runners, and deployment strategies.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ci-cd-and-automation
frontmatter_yaml.description: Automates CI/CD pipeline setup. Use when setting up or modifying build and deployment pipelines. Use when you need to automate quality gates, configure test runners in CI, or establish deployment strategies.

name: code-review-and-quality
description_raw: Conducts multi-axis code review. Use before merging any change. Use when reviewing code written by yourself, another agent, or a human. Use when you need to assess code quality across multiple dimensions before it enters the main branch.
description_normalized: Conducts multi-axis code review (correctness, readability, architecture, security, performance) before any change is merged.

frontmatter_yaml.name: code-review-and-quality
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conducts multi-axis code review. Use before merging any change. Use when reviewing code written by yourself, another agent, or a human. Use when you need to assess code quality across multiple dimensions before it enters the main branch.

name: code-simplification
description_raw: Simplifies code for clarity. Use when refactoring code for clarity without changing behavior. Use when code works but is harder to read, maintain, or extend than it should be. Use when reviewing code that has accumulated unnecessary complexity.
description_normalized: Reduces code complexity while preserving exact behavior, targeting readability over line-count reduction.

frontmatter_yaml.name: code-simplification
frontmatter_yaml.description: Simplifies code for clarity. Use when refactoring code for clarity without changing behavior. Use when code works but is harder to read, maintain, or extend than it should be. Use when reviewing code that has accumulated unnecessary complexity.

name: context-engineering
description_raw: Optimizes agent context setup. Use when starting a new session, when agent output quality degrades, when switching between tasks, or when you need to configure rules files and context for a project.
description_normalized: Curates what context an agent receives, when it receives it, and how it's structured to maximize output quality.

frontmatter_yaml.name: context-engineering
frontmatter_yaml.description: Optimizes agent context setup. Use when starting a new session, when agent output quality degrades, when switching between tasks, or when you need to configure rules files and context for a project.

name: debugging-and-error-recovery
description_raw: Guides systematic root-cause debugging. Use when tests fail, builds break, behavior doesn't match expectations, or you encounter any unexpected error. Use when you need a systematic approach to finding and fixing the root cause rather than guessing.
description_normalized: Provides a structured triage process for root-cause debugging when tests fail, builds break, or runtime behavior is unexpected.

frontmatter_yaml.name: debugging-and-error-recovery
frontmatter_yaml.description: Guides systematic root-cause debugging. Use when tests fail, builds break, behavior doesn't match expectations, or you encounter any unexpected error. Use when you need a systematic approach to finding and fixing the root cause rather than guessing.

name: deprecation-and-migration
description_raw: Manages deprecation and migration. Use when removing old systems, APIs, or features. Use when migrating users from one implementation to another. Use when deciding whether to maintain or sunset existing code.
description_normalized: Manages safe removal of old systems and migration of users from deprecated to new implementations.

frontmatter_yaml.name: deprecation-and-migration
frontmatter_yaml.description: Manages deprecation and migration. Use when removing old systems, APIs, or features. Use when migrating users from one implementation to another. Use when deciding whether to maintain or sunset existing code.

name: documentation-and-adrs
description_raw: Records decisions and documentation. Use when making architectural decisions, changing public APIs, shipping features, or when you need to record context that future engineers and agents will need to understand the codebase.
description_normalized: Records architectural decisions and context (why, not just what) via ADRs so future engineers and agents can understand the codebase.

frontmatter_yaml.name: documentation-and-adrs
frontmatter_yaml.description: Records decisions and documentation. Use when making architectural decisions, changing public APIs, shipping features, or when you need to record context that future engineers and agents will need to understand the codebase.

name: doubt-driven-development
description_raw: Subjects every non-trivial decision to a fresh-context adversarial review before it stands. Use when correctness matters more than speed, when working in unfamiliar code, when stakes are high (production, security-sensitive logic, irreversible operations), or any time a confident output would be cheaper to verify now than to debug later.
description_normalized: Materializes a fresh-context adversarial reviewer biased to disprove before any non-trivial decision stands.

frontmatter_yaml.name: doubt-driven-development
frontmatter_yaml.description: Subjects every non-trivial decision to a fresh-context adversarial review before it stands. Use when correctness matters more than speed, when working in unfamiliar code, when stakes are high (production, security-sensitive logic, irreversible operations), or any time a confident output would be cheaper to verify now than to debug later.

name: frontend-ui-engineering
description_raw: Builds production-quality UIs. Use when building or modifying user-facing interfaces. Use when creating components, implementing layouts, managing state, or when the output needs to look and feel production-quality rather than AI-generated.
description_normalized: Builds accessible, performant, design-system-adherent UIs that look professionally crafted rather than AI-generated.

frontmatter_yaml.name: frontend-ui-engineering
frontmatter_yaml.description: Builds production-quality UIs. Use when building or modifying user-facing interfaces. Use when creating components, implementing layouts, managing state, or when the output needs to look and feel production-quality rather than AI-generated.

name: git-workflow-and-versioning
description_raw: Structures git workflow practices. Use when making any code change. Use when committing, branching, resolving conflicts, or when you need to organize work across multiple parallel streams.
description_normalized: Enforces disciplined git workflow (trunk-based, atomic commits, safe branching) as the safety net for AI-accelerated development.

frontmatter_yaml.name: git-workflow-and-versioning
frontmatter_yaml.description: Structures git workflow practices. Use when making any code change. Use when committing, branching, resolving conflicts, or when you need to organize work across multiple parallel streams.

name: idea-refine
description_raw: Refines raw ideas into sharp, actionable concepts through structured divergent and convergent thinking. Use when an idea is still vague, when you need to stress-test assumptions before committing to a plan, or when you want to expand options before converging on one. Triggers on "ideate", "refine this idea", or "stress-test my plan".
description_normalized: Turns vague ideas into sharp, actionable concepts via structured divergent (expand) then convergent (stress-test, converge) thinking.

frontmatter_yaml.name: idea-refine
frontmatter_yaml.description: Refines raw ideas into sharp, actionable concepts through structured divergent and convergent thinking. Use when an idea is still vague, when you need to stress-test assumptions before committing to a plan, or when you want to expand options before converging on one. Triggers on "ideate", "refine this idea", or "stress-test my plan".

name: incremental-implementation
description_raw: Delivers changes incrementally. Use when implementing any feature or change that touches more than one file. Use when you're about to write a large amount of code at once, or when a task feels too big to land in one step.
description_normalized: Implements features in thin vertical slices — build one piece, test it, verify it, then expand — never writing large batches before testing.

frontmatter_yaml.name: incremental-implementation
frontmatter_yaml.description: Delivers changes incrementally. Use when implementing any feature or change that touches more than one file. Use when you're about to write a large amount of code at once, or when a task feels too big to land in one step.

name: interview-me
description_raw: Extracts what the user actually wants instead of what they think they should want. Achieves this through one-question-at-a-time interview until ~95% confidence about the underlying intent. Use when an ask is underspecified, when the user explicitly invokes, or when you catch yourself silently filling in ambiguous requirements before any plan, spec, or code exists.
description_normalized: Closes the gap between what users ask for and what they actually want via one-question-at-a-time interview before any plan or code begins.

frontmatter_yaml.name: interview-me
frontmatter_yaml.description: Extracts what the user actually wants instead of what they think they should want. Achieves this through one-question-at-a-time interview until ~95% confidence about the underlying intent. Use when an ask is underspecified ("build me X" without "for whom" or "why now"), when the user explicitly invokes ("interview me", "grill me", "are we sure?", "stress-test my thinking"), or when you catch yourself silently filling in ambiguous requirements before any plan, spec, or code exists.

name: performance-optimization
description_raw: Optimizes application performance. Use when performance requirements exist, when you suspect performance regressions, or when Core Web Vitals or load times need improvement. Use when profiling reveals bottlenecks that need fixing.
description_normalized: Measures then fixes performance bottlenecks — Core Web Vitals, load times, SLAs — never optimizing before profiling proves a problem exists.

frontmatter_yaml.name: performance-optimization
frontmatter_yaml.description: Optimizes application performance. Use when performance requirements exist, when you suspect performance regressions, or when Core Web Vitals or load times need improvement. Use when profiling reveals bottlenecks that need fixing.

name: planning-and-task-breakdown
description_raw: Breaks work into ordered tasks. Use when you have a spec or clear requirements and need to break work into implementable tasks. Use when a task feels too large to start, when you need to estimate scope, or when parallel work is possible.
description_normalized: Decomposes specs into small, verifiable tasks with acceptance criteria and explicit dependency ordering.

frontmatter_yaml.name: planning-and-task-breakdown
frontmatter_yaml.description: Breaks work into ordered tasks. Use when you have a spec or clear requirements and need to break work into implementable tasks. Use when a task feels too large to start, when you need to estimate scope, or when parallel work is possible.

name: security-and-hardening
description_raw: Hardens code against vulnerabilities. Use when handling user input, authentication, data storage, or external integrations. Use when building any feature that accepts untrusted data, manages user sessions, or interacts with third-party services.
description_normalized: Applies security-first practices to any feature touching user input, authentication, data storage, or external integrations — treating all external input as hostile.

frontmatter_yaml.name: security-and-hardening
frontmatter_yaml.description: Hardens code against vulnerabilities. Use when handling user input, authentication, data storage, or external integrations. Use when building any feature that accepts untrusted data, manages user sessions, or interacts with third-party services.

name: shipping-and-launch
description_raw: Prepares production launches. Use when preparing to deploy to production. Use when you need a pre-launch checklist, when setting up monitoring, when planning a staged rollout, or when you need a rollback strategy.
description_normalized: Runs a pre-launch checklist ensuring any production deployment is reversible, observable, and incremental with a rollback plan.

frontmatter_yaml.name: shipping-and-launch
frontmatter_yaml.description: Prepares production launches. Use when preparing to deploy to production. Use when you need a pre-launch checklist, when setting up monitoring, when planning a staged rollout, or when you need a rollback strategy.

name: source-driven-development
description_raw: Grounds every implementation decision in official documentation. Use when you want authoritative, source-cited code free from outdated patterns. Use when building with any framework or library where correctness matters.
description_normalized: Grounds every framework-specific implementation decision in official documentation with source citations, preventing stale training-data patterns.

frontmatter_yaml.name: source-driven-development
frontmatter_yaml.description: Grounds every implementation decision in official documentation. Use when you want authoritative, source-cited code free from outdated patterns. Use when building with any framework or library where correctness matters.

name: spec-driven-development
description_raw: Creates specs before coding. Use when starting a new project, feature, or significant change and no specification exists yet. Use when requirements are unclear, ambiguous, or only exist as a vague idea.
description_normalized: Writes a structured specification as shared source of truth before any code is written, treating unspecced code as guessing.

frontmatter_yaml.name: spec-driven-development
frontmatter_yaml.description: Creates specs before coding. Use when starting a new project, feature, or significant change and no specification exists yet. Use when requirements are unclear, ambiguous, or only exist as a vague idea.

name: test-driven-development
description_raw: Drives development with tests. Use when implementing any logic, fixing any bug, or changing any behavior. Use when you need to prove that code works, when a bug report arrives, or when you're about to modify existing functionality.
description_normalized: Writes failing tests before implementation, treating tests as proof — not optional verification — for every logic change or bug fix.

frontmatter_yaml.name: test-driven-development
frontmatter_yaml.description: Drives development with tests. Use when implementing any logic, fixing any bug, or changing any behavior. Use when you need to prove that code works, when a bug report arrives, or when you're about to modify existing functionality.

name: using-agent-skills
description_raw: Discovers and invokes agent skills. Use when starting a session or when you need to discover which skill applies to the current task. This is the meta-skill that governs how all other skills are discovered and invoked.
description_normalized: Meta-skill injected at session start that governs skill discovery and invocation across the entire agent-skills collection.

frontmatter_yaml.name: using-agent-skills
frontmatter_yaml.description: Discovers and invokes agent skills. Use when starting a session or when you need to discover which skill applies to the current task. This is the meta-skill that governs how all other skills are discovered and invoked.

name: code-reviewer
description_raw: Senior code reviewer that evaluates changes across five dimensions — correctness, readability, architecture, security, and performance. Use for thorough code review before merge.
description_normalized: Staff-Engineer-level persona that evaluates code across five quality dimensions and returns categorized, actionable feedback before merge.

frontmatter_yaml.name: code-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Senior code reviewer that evaluates changes across five dimensions — correctness, readability, architecture, security, and performance. Use for thorough code review before merge.

name: security-auditor
description_raw: Security engineer focused on vulnerability detection, threat modeling, and secure coding practices. Use for security-focused code review, threat analysis, or hardening recommendations.
description_normalized: Security-engineer persona focused on exploitable vulnerabilities, threat modeling, and practical hardening recommendations.

frontmatter_yaml.name: security-auditor
frontmatter_yaml.description: Security engineer focused on vulnerability detection, threat modeling, and secure coding practices. Use for security-focused code review, threat analysis, or hardening recommendations.

name: test-engineer
description_raw: QA engineer specialized in test strategy, test writing, and coverage analysis. Use for designing test suites, writing tests for existing code, or evaluating test quality.
description_normalized: QA-engineer persona that designs test suites, writes tests for existing code, and identifies coverage gaps.

frontmatter_yaml.name: test-engineer
frontmatter_yaml.description: QA engineer specialized in test strategy, test writing, and coverage analysis. Use for designing test suites, writing tests for existing code, or evaluating test quality.

name: spec
description_raw: Start spec-driven development — write a structured specification before writing code
description_normalized: Orchestration entry point that invokes the spec-driven-development skill to write a structured specification before any code begins.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Start spec-driven development — write a structured specification before writing code

name: plan
description_raw: Break work into small verifiable tasks with acceptance criteria and dependency ordering
description_normalized: Orchestration entry point that invokes planning-and-task-breakdown to decompose a spec into verifiable, ordered implementation tasks.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Break work into small verifiable tasks with acceptance criteria and dependency ordering

name: build
description_raw: Implement the next task incrementally — build, test, verify, commit
description_normalized: Orchestration entry point that invokes incremental-implementation and test-driven-development together to build and verify the next planned task.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Implement the next task incrementally — build, test, verify, commit

name: test
description_raw: Run TDD workflow — write failing tests, implement, verify. For bugs, use the Prove-It pattern.
description_normalized: Orchestration entry point that invokes test-driven-development for new features (write-fail-implement-verify) and the Prove-It pattern for bugs.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Run TDD workflow — write failing tests, implement, verify. For bugs, use the Prove-It pattern.

name: review
description_raw: Conduct a five-axis code review — correctness, readability, architecture, security, performance
description_normalized: Orchestration entry point that invokes code-review-and-quality to evaluate staged or recent changes across five quality axes.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Conduct a five-axis code review — correctness, readability, architecture, security, performance

name: code-simplify
description_raw: Simplify code for clarity and maintainability — reduce complexity without changing behavior
description_normalized: Orchestration entry point that invokes code-simplification to reduce complexity while preserving exact behavior.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Simplify code for clarity and maintainability — reduce complexity without changing behavior

name: ship
description_raw: Run the pre-launch checklist via parallel fan-out to specialist personas, then synthesize a go/no-go decision
description_normalized: Fan-out orchestrator that runs code-reviewer, security-auditor, and test-engineer in parallel then merges reports into a go/no-go shipping decision.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Run the pre-launch checklist via parallel fan-out to specialist personas, then synthesize a go/no-go decision
